source.recording: Matthew Harrison and Max Riemer - 2026_04_28 14_30 EDT - Recording.mp4
source.notes: Matthew Harrison and Max Riemer - 2026_04_28 14_30 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Max Riemer (Optimus) ran a discovery-and-demo call with Matthew Harrison and his colleague Johnny of Energy Logistics Management, a ~20-person non-asset freight brokerage that dropped ZoomInfo over poor response rates and inflexible contracts and now wants to rebuild a 'smile and dial' sales team. Max scoped their operation, then positioned Optimus's transportation/lane data as the differentiator at $750/month for unlimited seats, offering a 90-day exit clause to de-risk the commitment. Matthew is engaged and will review with ownership; the agreed next steps are Max sending a PDF proposal and a decision by end of week / early next week, with a tentative June start.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max scoped the business before pitching and repeatedly built on what Matthew actually said (e.g., echoing his ZoomInfo lock-in frustration with his own Seamless story), rather than reciting a script; acoustics support this, with pitch variation 9.44 st (z=+1.0 above his own baseline) and steadier volume (CV z=-0.6).

evidence.quote: before I sort of go on about Optimus, can you sort of give me the uh the highle overview of energy logistics
you said it. Uh we have emails, phone numbers, all that fun stuff. But it's also that transportation related data
We uh we lost a potential client because of that with Seamless. Same thing.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could restate it: Optimus adds real transportation/lane data so reps show they recognize a shipper's operation, for a flat $750/month with unlimited seats, and one landed customer pays for it.

evidence.quote: it's also that transportation related data that's going to make a shipping manager or a supply chain procurement, whoever you're reaching out to, you know, really stop in their tracks
at the 750. So that just also that's unlimited seats, unlimited usage. There's no credits or anything like that.
you land one or two customers, it's Optimus paid for.

relevance.explanation: Max tailored the demo to this prospect—asking for a real lane and running St. Louis outbound (Matthew's own market) to surface actual shippers—rather than showing a generic profile.

evidence.quote: what's a good lane for you guys off the top of your head?
So, if we do St. Louis, we'll just stick with outbound for now.
We come down to Taylor Farms. Looks like they got two lanes out of St. Louis, Dallas, Texas, and SMA, Tennessee.

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked productive, buying-relevant questions (staff structure, CRM, ZoomInfo seats/price, pain points) and secured next steps—a PDF proposal and a decision timeline—but the follow-up was left buyer-driven rather than a booked meeting.

evidence.quote: So really there's there's no one that's like dedicated to sales right now.
a proposal just with everything sort of laid out so y'all can take that to them
I'm going to put a June start date on there. Basically, it's the first Monday of every month.

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Before ending the call, lock a specific dated follow-up—e.g., 'Let's hold Thursday at 2pm to review the proposal after you've spoken with ownership'—instead of leaving it as 'we'll be back in touch.'

signals.signal: Requested a formal proposal to bring to ownership
signals.quote: If you want to send over a proposal, that'd be that'd be great.

signals.signal: Framed the deal as low-risk and committed to circling back
signals.quote: obviously with, you know, the 90-day clause, I don't think there's, you know, high risk in, you know, at least those first 90 days
It'll probably be by the end of this week, but I do have to take a trip um early next week.

signals.signal: Affirmed the core value prop unprompted
signals.quote: you're focused on transportation. It's not, you know, it's not doubling as a software for purchasing managers

signals.signal: Asked one purchase-mechanics question, indicating some buying consideration
signals.quote: one thing regarding the billing like you will charge per month, right? Or like it is uh it would be annual payment

signals.signal: Opened by admitting the call was not a priority
signals.quote: I'm sorry. We totally forgot about this call.

signals.signal: Declined to engage when explicitly invited
